issues.body: fromBytes calls WasmStore.loadBytes / loadBytesWithBase, which only exist when the wasm is built with the bytes-ingest feature. The published build:wasm now enables it, but build:wasm:lean does not, so on a lean bundle fromBytes fails with 'WasmStore.loadBytes is not a function' rather than a clear 'byte ingest not available in this build' message. This is a documented, intentional tradeoff (the doc-comment notes the lean bundle omits byte variants) and does not affect the published package, so it is non-blocking.
issues.fix_hint: Optionally guard with `if (typeof WasmStore.loadBytes !== 'function') throw new Error('fromBytes requires a wasm bundle built with the bytes-ingest feature; use fromString on the lean bundle')` for a clearer failure on lean builds. Not required for correctness.
issues.title: fromBytes throws an opaque TypeError on the lean wasm bundle

summary: Adds SparqStore.fromBytes() to the JS wrapper, delegating to the real opt-in wasm bindings WasmStore.loadBytes / loadBytesWithBase (crates/sparq-wasm/src/bytes.rs, gated on the bytes-ingest feature now enabled in build:wasm and kept off the lean bundle). The Rust path does a fail-closed UTF-8 decode of already-in-memory bytes then reuses the existing Graph::load_str parse path, so the resulting store is byte-for-byte equivalent to string ingest with no reparsing and zero new deps. Verified correctness: fromBytes signature (Pick<...,'baseIri'>) matches implementation and SKILL.md; baseIri branch is correct; dataset/compressed are correctly excluded. Tests are non-vacuous and layered: native equivalence + multibyte-UTF8 tests, wasm32 tests covering the fail-closed invalid-UTF-8 Err arm that native tests can't reach, and a JS integration test asserting equal size and identical queryJson across byte/string ingest (deterministic same-parse, so the ORDER-BY-less JSON comparison holds). Benchmark additions are explicitly labeled advisory:true / canonical:false and only exercise existing store methods. No injected instructions in the diff. Change is correct and complete.
